entries.content: Stoicism is an ancient Greek philosophy founded by Zeno of Citium around 300 BCE. Stoics believe in living according to virtue, accepting what we cannot control, and focusing on what we can - our thoughts and actions. Key principles include wisdom, justice, courage, and temperance.

metadata.key_figures: Marcus Aurelius, Epictetus, Seneca, Zeno of Citium
metadata.core_teachings: Virtue is the highest good, control what you can, accept what you cannot
metadata.practices: Negative visualization, morning reflection, evening review
metadata.modern_relevance: Cognitive behavioral therapy, resilience training, mindfulness

entries.content: The Stoics were philosophers who followed Stoicism, teaching that virtue, reason, and living in harmony with nature lead to a good life. Famous Stoics include Marcus Aurelius (Roman Emperor), Epictetus (former slave), and Seneca (advisor to Nero). They emphasized practical wisdom and emotional resilience.

metadata.key_idea: The dichotomy of control - focus only on what's in your power

entries.content: Existentialism emphasizes individual existence, freedom, and the search for meaning in an apparently meaningless universe. Key thinkers include Sartre, Camus, and Kierkegaard. The philosophy asserts that 'existence precedes essence' - we create our own meaning through choices and actions.

metadata.key_concepts: Authenticity, bad faith, absurdity, freedom, responsibility
metadata.famous_quote: Man is condemned to be free - Sartre
